context: STATE_JSON describes a Next.js + Convex + Better Auth + Biome + shadcn + Vitest stack with libraries=['zustand'] and ai='vercel-ai-sdk'. (No Drizzle, no Effect, no Prisma, no Rust.)
expected_behavior: Emits a markdown bullet group for each matched signal: Next.js, Convex, Better Auth, Biome, shadcn, Vitest, Zustand, Vercel AI SDK
Does NOT emit groups for signals NOT in the state: no Drizzle, no Prisma, no Effect, no Rust
Uses '#### <Stack name>' as the heading for each group (matches sync_bruhs_block.py's render_rules_body expectations)
Each rule is one bullet, short, and prescriptive — does not include backstory or rationale
Output is stable across runs with the same state — same order, same wording, byte-identical
Recognizes synonyms — 'Next.js' (with the dot) and 'nextjs' both map to the Next.js rule group; 'Tailwind CSS' maps to 'tailwind'; '@tanstack/react-query' maps to 'tanstack-query'
Recognizes animation synonyms — 'framer-motion' and 'motion' both map to one Motion / Animation rule group
Exits 0 even when no signals match (empty stack) — emits empty stdout, NOT an error
Invalid stdin JSON exits 2 with a stderr error, NOT 0 with empty output
--json flag emits {signals, matched} structure for debugging
failure_modes: Emits the Drizzle rule when the state doesn't mention drizzle anywhere
Skips Convex even though state.database includes 'convex'
Order shifts between runs — output is non-deterministic
Includes meta-commentary or backstory in rule bullets instead of just the prescriptive rule
Treats 'Next.js' and 'nextjs' as different signals and emits the rule group twice
Exits 0 on malformed JSON instead of exit 2
Hard-codes a 'universal' rule group regardless of stack — violates the 'only stack-derived' design
